details:
Native linux + windows jobs fail fleet-wide on two gameplay walkthrough tests
for the multilevel-campaign maps added by #1301, reddening native CI for every
native PR (e.g. PRs #1342, #1349 native jobs):

1. test_stdio_map_walkthrough_gravemoor (McpServerTest) / _mcp_walkthrough_gravemoor:
   "Expected object handle for gravemoorStart" -> _mcp_get_object_by_name returns
   None. The object IS defined in content (res/maps/gravemoor/map.json name+type
   "gravemoorStart"; res/maps/gravemoor/config.json "gravemoorStart" entry), so
   this is a RUNTIME object-handle/registration issue, not missing content: the
   named object is not resolvable by name via the MCP handle path at runtime.

2. test_map_walkthrough_hearthfall (GameTest) / walkthrough_hearthfall_map:
   "Arrival should play the Hearthfall intro" -> game_map.getBoolProperty(
   "hearthfall_intro") is False after arrival. The intro logic EXISTS
   (res/maps/hearthfall/script.py line ~38-40 gates on and sets
   "hearthfall_intro"), so the trigger that should invoke it on map arrival is
   not firing. Runtime trigger-wiring issue, not missing content.

Both are native-validated (the walkthrough tests require the built _game engine),
so a fix cannot be validated in a submodule-restricted sandbox; whoever has a
native build should debug the gravemoor object-name registration and the
hearthfall arrival/onEnter intro trigger. Prior fleet-wide gameplay red was
fixed by #1274; this is a distinct, NEW red introduced with the #1301 maps.

evidence.failure: Expected object handle for gravemoorStart -> None
Arrival should play the Hearthfall intro; hearthfall_intro False after arrival

evidence.note: distinct new red from the #1274-fixed gameplay red

impact: Every native PR's linux+windows jobs fail on gravemoor/hearthfall walkthroughs (NEW red distinct from #1274's fix); native CI cannot go green for any PR until the two #1301-map runtime bugs are fixed
suggestedImprovement: With a native build: fix gravemoorStart object-name handle resolution (defined in map.json/config.json but _mcp_get_object_by_name returns None) and the hearthfall arrival intro trigger (script sets hearthfall_intro but it is False after arrival)
summary: New multilevel maps gravemoor/hearthfall fail native walkthrough tests (runtime object-handle + arrival-trigger bugs, content present), reddening native linux+windows fleet-wide
